title: Add skill-gap audit log to pull-based skill loading
description: When Mira's agents load skills for a task (pull-based), log both what was loaded AND what was available but skipped. This makes the 'never went looking' failure mode visible. Currently, if the agent's meta-cognition is wrong — it thinks it has enough context but silently missed a relevant skill — there is no trace of what was overlooked. Adding a skipped-skills log turns a silent miss into a detectable signal.
rationale: The reading note's core insight: pull-based retrieval bets on agent meta-cognition. RAG failures show up as 'retrieved the wrong thing'; pull failures show up as 'never searched at all' — which is harder to detect because nothing in the log looks wrong. Mira's skill-loading is pull-based: the agent decides which skills to inject. If the agent's self-model is miscalibrated for a task type, it proceeds with a silent gap. The reading note asks: 'which error is harder to find?' — the answer is the pull miss, because it leaves no trace.
diff_description: In the skill-loading path (likely sub_agent.py or soul_manager.py where skills are selected and injected into prompts): after the agent selects skills for a task, add a log line of the form: SKILL_AUDIT task_type={X} loaded={n} available={m} skipped_categories={[...]}. The skipped_categories field lists skill tag buckets that were available but not selected. This does not change behavior — it only adds observability. Over time, if certain task types consistently skip entire skill categories, that signals a meta-cognition gap worth addressing. Implementation: before injecting the selected skills, compute set(all_skill_tags) - set(selected_skill_tags) and log the difference alongside the task label.
source_note: 2026-04-04_1-拉取式信息获取是对-agent-元认知的一次赌注.md
